Review: We went again and sat at the bar this time, I had 5 pints of guinness and not one buy-back, I ordered a basket of onion rings and there were about 5 in the basket, the rest was filled with crumbs, the chili was not even edible.
The food was good, but it's not worth the wait--or the lousy service.
After hearing all of the specials, you would think they were twice as much as the menu prices (as other places do).
When we sat down, the waiter barely looked in our direction and abruptly shoved our menus on the table.
they continue to expand their garden space, w/out expanding their kitchen and cannot provide even adequate service.
Extremely RUDE servers who intially took our order and never returned with drinks or to see if we required anythign else.
1) Service is very slow and unattentive (10 minutes for our server to come by with our menus and to take our drink order, another 10 to bring the drinks/take our dinner order, you get the idea.
the waiters had a really hard time remembering to bring drinks, and when they did they were not what was ordered.
It is now nothing but an overpriced establishment that is shamelessly riding on the coat tails of its past.
We arrived at 5:30 and had leave at 7:30 without dessert or coffee because the kitchen took insanely long between courses.

Having dined there over a half-dozen I have tried everything on the menu but depending on what night, time and who is taking your order, be prepared for loud, smokey (it is a bar afterall) and herbed waitstaff.

The drinks, served with a little extra in the shaker, anticipate the aptly executed menu, which emphasizes comfort foods of a bygone era--veggies, for example, include crisp Brussels sprouts, shredded beets and bubbling-hot scalloped potatoes.
Needless to say, we weren't suprised when our server told us he never put our appetizer order in.
I ordered chicken vindaloo, the delivery took about an hour, and it cost me $13 with tip.
The atmosphere was wonderful, however the service and food were not.
no dish on brunch menu over $11 - definitely worth the price.
Don't know about the rest of the food, but the burgers are one of New York's hidden treats.

Yes, hard not to look at the waitresses (and they're really good servers), but as I'm reminded by everyone who knows Hooters, they don't rank anywhere near the girls at the Hooters locations South of the ol' Mason-Dixon.

From a solid homey, affordable Italian menu in a warm, exhileratingly crowded location they've gone to a 30$ minimum per person on opera nights with a quality of food and drink that just does not warrant the prices.
The space is a bit cramped and the waitress seemed to avoid eye contact at all costs, however, I would recommend you go just for the food.
for instance, i sat next to this couple who experienced problems with their food and the asked to speak to a manager.
The piano guy isn't there all the time, but when he is it's a great addition to the meal.
Service was good throughout the dinner but when it came time to get the check, we waited over 20 min.
One friend had the steak which he didn't care for, but after waiting so long to be seated he was hungry.
The waiter set down my companion's meal and didn't return with my dish for some time.
After admitting that the items on the bill were not in line with the menu, the manager reserved the right to price at her own discretion!
Ten minutes later the manager relented and dropped off a glass of water with an upturned nose.
they waited to get us water after they took our food orders, which took the waitstaff a long time to do.
The Food The owners' visits to farmers' markets in Brooklyn drive the ingredient-centric New American menu, which changes about once a month.
After scrunching into the tiny bar area and ordering a drink, I was annoyed but couldn't help but notice the handsome bartender having a tete-a-tete with another French-speaking cutie.
Fried appetizers, tuna steak and other dishes are also available, as well as an assortment of sakes, graphed on the menu according to flavor components.
The place was empty with three waiters working and it still took 15 minutes to bring us menus.
Our waitress kept forgetting our drinks.
the service was horrible then and the food was decent.
The place is not meant for in house dining, but if you want to grab a meal on the go, this is the perfect choice.
The bar area was fairly crowded but service remained friendly and efficient.
Waitress was nice but didnt know some specials and was alittle off, think she was new.
Yes it's pricey for counter service, sawdust floors and deafening chatter but the pastrami makes it all worth it.
For the prices, the food is decidedly lackluster, and the service, unless you are a regular, can be quite rude.
the waitress never asked us how the food was or if we needed anything.
My complaint was with the service-- our waitress was rather pushy and visibly annoyed when we didn't order appetizers or dessert.
The bartender virtually ignored us while we waited, took 15 minutes to get drinks.
The only saving grace is the sweet tea - but even that isn't worth the wait.
Then the waitress became upset when she returned with the drinks and we didn't stop our conversations quick enough to remind her who had ordered what only 5 minutes prior.
Oh, and kudos to the hostess who appeared out of thin air with extra napkins just moments after a glass of water was knocked over - she's upping the bar on service here.
I especially recommend ordering the duck, foie gras, and beef cheeks although it's probably hard to go wrong with anything on the menu.
The noise here is so bad that people entered and left without ordering and they lowered the lights during the dinner.
Sure, the burgers are good, but not good enough to make up for the abominable service and disgusting, filthy atmosphere.
The waiter appeared promptly, took our order, bought us drinks and was never to be seen again.
Our waitress had no clue about the menu and (after we had ordered a bottle of wine) told us the kitchen was closing and stood guard until we ordered.
The brunch entrees were shockingly overpriced, even for the area, and especially given the food quality.
Staff wouldn't take no for an answer on pitchers of sangria.
I went here all 4 years that I lived in the area, and being a not-so-loaded student, it was a great place to go for some good sushi and a very pleasant comforting atmosphere.
service was attentive at the beginning but the waiter lost us towards the end and we had to flag them down for the check.
Then the waiter came back for our food order at 10, and we hadn't received our drinks yet.
After all that, it is average food at best with huge bill at the end.
when I came back for brunch a week or so later, I was told that the marscapone version was no longer being served, because there was a new cook.
The Food Though much of the menu draws from border countries with the likes of pasta, cordon bleu and schnitzel, dishes that involve cheese are best.
D, who can't decide on a single dish, the tapas menu allowed me to express my true culinary self.
We weren't even offered a dinner menu and the waitress didn't even notice that we wanted drinks until after I ordered them from the bar myself.
The food was good overall, but unremarkable given the price.
On the top of everything at the end of the dinner the waiter gave us the wrong check.
The Food There's a Mediterranean bent to the menu--hummus, babaganoush and stuffed grape leaves are offered, as well as Moroccan chicken and spicy Merguez lamb sausage.

When we finally ordered our wine, the waitress brought the bottle of wine over with ONE GLASS MISSING - meaning, we'd have to wait even longer for the final glass to arrive - which were all piping hot from the dishwasher.
The last time I was there (last week) our food took a little too long and a manager promptly apologized and offered to comp our desserts (not expected but appreciated).
I called to confirm the reservations on the morning of the lunch and the hostess had the reservations set for 12:30pm which I quickly corrected.
Our waiter was a little stressed and brought a couple things out late and as a result, he apologized graciously and gave us wine on the house to compensate for the delay.
Try the Bocadillos to start and don't forget to order a Mojito from the bar (they're the best!
The waitress came back with the bill and money (with a calculator) and asked for more tips.
If it's just a quick martini at the bar (which I recommend Jeffery's) or a mind blowing Roast Chicken, go to Village!
For dessert the waiter reccomended his favorite which was a Torreja.
Thanks to waiter I learned so much about wine too.
Each week, Zutto's sushi chef never ceases to amaze me with new concoctions.
Our waitress forgot to bring me another margarita, but somehow she managed to get it on the bill.
We went to the Sunday brunch, and were pleasantly surprised to find a jazz combo there.
my husband has certain food allergies, and they were 100% accomodating even for the tasting menu which is seven courses for $50 - they even substituted dishes for ones that he couldn't have.
Chef Shea Gallante takes up residence in the former kitchen of Waxman, now walled off from the diners but visible through glass service doors.
We were given only one waiter for 20 people, and it honestly took over 1/2 an hour to get coffee (I actully went across the street to Dunkin Donuts!)

(And the mixed steamed seafood appetizer took *forever* to arrive -- apparently the oysters here are shucked by machine, and they only have one shucker, so if more than one table orders anything off of that section of the menu, you'll be waiting a while.

I came to celebrate finishing my third year of med school and was greatly disappointed by the way I was treated at the door (rude and terse), the food (mediocre), and the ambiance (very loud, crowded, and uncomfortable close to our neighbors despite the large room).
Between the 6 of us we tried almost everything on the menu and we were all very pleased with our meals.
the decor could be a bit better, and if there was a small bar the overall atmosphere would be a bit more inviting (especially for regulars).
for the prices that are on the regular menu we were expecting something more upscale and less generic.
The waitress was slow and had to ask the kitchen first every time we asked her about anything on the menu.

The waitor never gave us the specials menu (which they gave to a another couple who sat next to us 20 minutes later) and the service was very slow even for the basics (bread and water, for which we had to ask a couple of times).
Although the restaurant was far from crowded (maybe three tables), service was abysmally slow, to the point that we wondered if the server was trying to avoid us.
They seem under staffed and Waiters do not really know the menu.
i also like their cocktail menu and dessert menu (try the black tea rose ice cream and the coconout waffles).
she never once checked on us after serving our drinks, we asked another waitress about our sandwiches, she attempted to check on them but didn't really get an answer.
But the wait staff's attitude will make me rethink about returning.

We took seats at the bar with 2 amazing bottles of wine (one for the appetizers and one to match the entrees) Monsieur Zucco was very accomodating and kept a sharp eye at filling our glasses at the right time at the right level.
from the hostess to the bartender to the waiter (who told us that we had to leave because the tables needed to be turned around more quickly even though it was a friends engagement dinner).
Our waiter did not come back once to reserve our wine.
We told the waitress we'd like the fries hot with our main courses but she only ordered more once the rest of the food had arrived so we didn't get them until we were half way through.
We received our food and coffee at the same time, and couldn't get the attention of any waiter to refill our cups.
The service was spotty as a couple of drink orders were forgotten and the waiter didn't really come to check on us.
Costs could be better - expect to be upsold by the hovering wait staff - and come for the authentic tastes, not the interior decor (looked a littel like Cheers!
The menu is short and sweet: hamburgers, cheeseburgers and double cheeseburgers, with all-beef patties steam-grilled on a pile of onions and served on square white buns with pickles.
I had the first pasta listed on the menu and it left me craving the taste again for days after.

To elaborate; the menu is written in French but the dishes themselves are something that your - enthusiastic yet talentless boyfriend - might muster up for you, leaving you looking concerned for his love for you.
THe bartendars were right on top of getting us our wine and setting up free blue cheese dip and chips for us.
True, the ambiance is something out of Goodfellas, but it the food and waiters are out of this world!
calle ocho is definitely high on my list, the food and mojitos are excellent (the sangria is a miss, flor de sol has much better sangria) but the service leaves much to be desired.
Once we ordered, it took at least 30 minutes to get our food, with our server no where in site.
The Food Skip the menu and head for the restaurant's bountiful and ever-changing vegan buffet for an abundance of affordable servings.
However, they do bring you a lot of food for your buck, and despite the unremarkable food it strikes me as a good place to fill up on the cheap for lunch or after a night of drinking.
Waited an hour for the food to come out, couldnt even find the waitress.
We could have made a meal of the yummy dumplings from the dumpling menu.
I checked this place out during the blizzard, and was thrilled to discover the $12 early bird price fixe (until 6:30 PM) -- appetizer, entree, dessert, and soda.
With 5 items on the menu and prices this low, you'd think turnover would be a priority -- think again!!!
Got the check tossed on table 10 minutes after being served and hostess came by to rush us some more.
It took us about 30 minutes to get seated, the burgers came without lettuce, they brought us the wrong drinks, and the flavor of the burgers were below average.
However when the party was complete we had the other waitress come over who said that we had to order all the food at once and couldnt order just appetizers.
Server was nonexistent; when we flagged her down, she told us that the food was 'being plated' when clearly they were just starting to prepare it.
We were so irritated about how horrible the food was, we didn't stick around to hangout at the bar.
Those appetizers only appeared after we complained to the manager.

When one compares the quality of the food to some of the city's other italian restaurants, and then particularly when one factors in the very reasonable prices here, I feel that this restaurant comes out a winner.
I did not like the place because: -it took the waiter over 20 min to take our order and then over an hour until we got our food.

I was lured in by the fact that they have soy options for almost everything that includes dairy on the menu, as well as tofu subs for eggs; It is hard to find mexican tinged food with an optional vegetarian slant.
And the owner didnt seem to care, she was more interested in talking at the bar than hearing from me.

On Friday night the busboy was sulky verging on rude he brought the fries (ordered for the entrees) twice before the appetizers came and then again the minute we'd finished them and looked put out to be asked to take the empty plates away.
I don't even think I would have minded waiting at the bar for over an hour if it weren't so crowded and if they had little tapas for the hungry people forced to wait!
Sure, the waitresses are hot, but you'll have to wait 30 minutes just to get your drinks.
Even when the chef is not in the house, the food and service are right on target.
I chose Guastavinos for my wedding reception dinner (for 19 of us) and planned it through their private dining staff.
Great decor, unfortunately the worst service possible.
This guy should work in a diner, didn't even know the menu, he couldn't explain me anything, a real nightmare.
The service was terrible- our waiter was unable to make any recommendations, mumbled the special and failed to check back during the course of our meal.
The handwritten menu makes me think the choices change frequently -- we started with pumpkin fritters and then had bunny rabbit and sea bass cakes.
Nothing on the menu jumped out at me, but when we tasted the chicken and the pork tenderloin.

Got a table during restaurant week for lunch and the service was cordial, if slower than usual.
Good food, but they could expand their desert menu.

Includes a diverse array of entrees ranging from Boar (prepared in your choice of either the French or Asian tradition), Mussels, or Pad Thai that grace the menu all at once, each one prepared in a distinctly different and delicate sauce.
The food is fantastic, Gourmet comfort food and has gotten progressively better over the past year, as did the service.
The waiter got aggravated when we explained how we wanted our entrees prepared.
My friend also knew the chef, so we had some other courses not on the menu, but even knowing the chef didn't get us the service I would expect at this type of restaurant.
We waited an hour for our appetizers, (I suspect the waitress forgot to put in our order because the restaurant was not busy) and then the entrees came at the same time!
Since we had to wait, we decided to get a drink but the bartender ignored us even when we made it known that we would like to order a drinks.
Sure, you can sit back and watch the celebrites pour in, or sit at the bar, and watch true masters create old world sushi, and new world rolls.
Although the tables may be closely situated, the candle-light, food-quality and service overcompensate.

After an hour of waiting at an overcrowded bar with our coats in our hands and no room to move, I approached the host (who never bothered to give us an update throughout our wait) to ask him how much longer, He was incredibly rude and dismissed me with his hand and walked away from me while I was trying to talk to him!
The raw bar that we were served was abyssmal, and our dinners of salmon (mine overcooked) and chicken (hers, very bland) weren't much better.

The service, which was horrendous - they charged us for two appetizers when we asked to split one (when we brought it up, he insisted that the appetizer really did only consist of 2 sliced tomatoes and 1 slice of cheese).
The menu offers a lineup of USDA prime steaks at higher-end prices paired with sides such as crab cakes, fried oysters and onion rings.
the place is small but everything else is 'big' in terms of quality variety and smiles of the wait service.

I don't claim to know enough about authentic Italian cuisine but the food is good, provided they have the menu item you order.

About the food we had a little difilculty keeping the orders straight ,the menu has too many choices,but the server was very helpul and suggested de paella valenciana which was extremele delicious.

As the night rolls on the place turns into a bar/lounge so the wait staff is constantly rushing you to eat up and move out, not the kind of atmosphere you want for the price you are paying for your entree.

This is a busy spot from 1130 AM to 130 PM M-F but you never have to wait more than 5 minutes for a table, unless you have a large party, thanks to fast and friendly service provided by the waitstaff and kitchen.

the food isn't spectacular, but I have yet to be disappointed in anything I've been served, and there's a definite attempt by the kitchen to keep the menu fresh and interesting.
My complaint was the service was not that good but the food made up for it.

The waiter, Momir, was on top of everything the whole time-not to mention entertaining us by constantly hitting on the two blond souther girl sitting at the table next to us-- and the free wine and after dinner espressos were much appreciated.
Full of characters from the neighborhood, it's a fun place to meet up with friends or have a drink at the bar.
